title: Quick Pressure-Cooker Vegetable Biryani

ingredients.name: basmati rice, soaked 20 minutes and drained

subs.name: coconut milk (3 tbsp) for a different flavour profile

ingredients.name: whole garam masala (bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves)

steps.text: Heat ghee in the pressure cooker base over medium-high flame. Add the whole garam masala ingredients (cinnamon stick 1 inch, 3 cardamom pods, 3 cloves, 1 bay leaf) and fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add sliced onion and fry until deep golden brown, about 7 minutes, stirring frequently. A well-browned onion is the base of all biryani flavour. Reserve half the fried onion to sprinkle on top later.

Add ginger-garlic paste to the remaining onion and fry for 1 minute. Add chopped tomato, red chilli powder, turmeric and garam masala. Stir and cook until the tomato is soft and the oil begins to separate, about 3 minutes.
Beat the curd with a pinch of salt and add to the pot. Stir over medium heat for 1 minute until the curd is absorbed. Add the diced potato and carrot, peas, mint and coriander and mix.

Add the soaked, drained rice and 2 cups of water (the water should be well-salted). Stir gently once. Secure the pressure cooker lid and cook on high until you get exactly 1 whistle, then immediately reduce to the lowest flame and cook for 5 more minutes.

Turn off the heat and let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes — do not force-release or the rice will clump. Open the lid, fluff gently, scatter the reserved fried onion on top and serve with raita and pickle.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: ghee → neutral oil; curd → coconut milk (3 tbsp) for a different flavour profile; peas → cauliflower florets or corn
